meta.name: self-explanatory-code-commenting
meta.title: Self Explanatory Code Commenting Skill
meta.description: Guidelines for GitHub Copilot to write comments to achieve self-explanatory code with less comments. Examples are in JavaScript but it should work on any language that has comments. Triggers on: **

rules.description: // GitHub API rate limit: 5000 requests/hour for authenticated users
// SECURITY: Validate input to prevent SQL injection before using in query
// Bad: Don't maintain history in comments
Remember: The best comment is the one you don't need to write because the code is self-documenting.

directives.description: Write code that speaks for itself. Comment only when necessary to explain WHY, not WHAT.
We do not need comments most of the time.
// Apply progressive tax brackets: 10% up to 10k, 20% above
1. Is the code self-explanatory? → No comment needed
2. Would a better variable/function name eliminate the need? → Refactor instead
4. Will this help future maintainers? → Good comment
Calculate compound interest using the standard formula.
